prompt: In Nature journal's Scientific Reports conference proceedings from 2012, in the article that did not mention plasmons or plasmonics, what nano-compound is studied? Don't use the prefix nano in your answer if there is one.
actual_output: The nano-compound studied is **gold particles** (gold nanoparticles, AuNPs). The article "Enhancing Nanoparticle-Based Visible Detection by Controlling the Extent of Aggregation" by Seokwon Lim et al. (Scientific Reports, 2012, DOI: 10.1038/srep00456) was the only one among the 4 nano/plasmon-related articles published in Scientific Reports in 2012 that did not mention plasmons or plasmonics. It focused on controlling the aggregation of colloidal gold nanoparticles for visible color-change-based detection of biological entities.
